comment_text.sn35.168:1.1: This chapter is titled saṭṭhipeyyālavagga, “The Chapter of Sixty Abbreviated Texts”. I follow MS, BJT, and Bodhi, which give the full count of sixty. The PTS edition, by combining each triad of discourses as one, numbers only 19 discourses up to 186. Its numbering remains out of sync for the remainder of this Saṁyutta.

root_text.sn35.168:1.1: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ, tatra vo chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.168:1.3: Cakkhu, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.168:1.4: jivhā aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.168:1.5: mano anicco; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.168:1.6: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ, tatra vo chando pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.168:1.1: “Mendicants, you should give up desire for what is impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.168:1.3: The eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind are impermanent:
translation_text.sn35.168:1.5: you should give up desire for them.
translation_text.sn35.168:1.6: You should give up desire for what is impermanent.”
